steps.content: Whales travel between 68 and 73 miles per day, so the daily range is $68 \le d \le 73$.
Over 14 days, total miles $x = 14d$. Multiply all parts of the inequality by 14 (positive, so signs unchanged): $68 \times 14 \le x \le 73 \times 14$.

• $68+14 \le x \le 73+14$: Adds 14 instead of multiplying — wrong operation.
  • $\left(68\right) \left(14\right) \le x \le \left(73\right) \left(14\right)$: $(68)(14) \le x \le (73)(14)$ — matches our result. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
  • $68 \le 14+x \le 73$: $68 \le 14 + x \le 73$ — subtracts 14 from $x$, which doesn't represent total distance.
  • D: $68 \le 14x \le 73$ — divides by 14 instead of multiplying, giving a tiny range.
